methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU partnering with AI Ready RVA signals distributed capacity-building through institutional anchors beyond NoVA, creating peer learning networks that could democratize deep AI fluency across Richmond's urban core and potentially surrounding counties.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents ground-up capability building in an economically transitioning region, testing whether distributed deep learning can take root in areas historically excluded from tech growth.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - pilotonline.com
signals.signal_reading: Hampton Roads businesses embracing AI suggests platform-delivered tools are enabling broad but shallow adoption in a defense-industrial economy, creating distributed usage without necessarily building indigenous development capacity.

signals.title: Northern Virginia Technology Council Urges Removal of Data Center Tax Repeal from State Budget - PR Newswire
signals.signal_reading: NoVA Technology Council lobbying on data center tax policy reveals how concentrated infrastructure investment deepens metro advantage through policy capture, reinforcing the capital-policy-talent feedback loop that maintains regional inequality.

signals.title: Prince William Co. school system establishes guidance on AI glasses
signals.signal_reading: Prince William schools establishing AI glasses guidance indicates consumer AI tools are penetrating institutions rapidly, but policy response focuses on managing shallow deployment rather than building deep technical literacy among students or educators.
